crochetPatternFluff.name: Bag of Holding
entries: One of the great challenges for any adventurer is paring down your {@item dungeoneer's pack|XPHB} to the true essentials. Fifty feet of {@item rope|XPHB}? {@item Caltrops|XPHB}? Climbing stakes? Whatever you choose to leave behind always seems to be the thing you wind up missing the most. (Yes, it is possible to crochet fifty feet of {@item rope|XPHB} if needed, but you don't want to do it, especially while being chased by an {@creature owlbear}!)

No wonder the {@item bag of holding}, with its nearly infinite storage capacity and magical maximum weight, is so cherished by adventurers in every realm. With one of these enchanted carryalls, you can bring along solutions to the most unlikely problems and still have plenty of space for brand-new treasures!

While you'll need to find a skilled enchanter to unlock its endless capacity, this pattern will set you on the path to stitching your very own {@item bag of holding}. The stylish design offers plenty of space for your daily adventuring gear, with a sturdy strap for easy carrying. Just be sure to follow the pattern carefully, lest you accidentally wind up with an unwanted {@item bag of devouring} instead.

images.title: Side Strip with ring attached.

crochetPatternFluff.name: Beholder
entries: A beholder's myriad eyes engage in relentless observation, filtering information through their powerful alien intelligence. Each of their ten eyestalks emits a different magical ray, none of them pleasant. Paired with the anti-magic cone they can cast from their large, central eye, it makes for an intensely unfair fight.

When possible, a solid strategy for fighting a {@creature beholder} is to give it something else to focus on that's not you. Flattery is another time-honored tactic, since most beholders have a planet-sized ego. This pattern can come in handy for both techniques. It's oversized for an amigurumi, because no mere miniature form could capture a beholder's true might. Anatomically correct and majestic in scope, it makes for a gift that's sure to stroke a beholder's inflated ego. It is also sure to delight a loved one—if you're lucky enough not to have a nearby beholder in need of placating.

crochetPatternFluff.name: Bracers of Archery
entries: One popular use of magic is allowing you to master a skill without devoting endless hours of practice and study to the effort. The {@item bracers of archery} are one example of this enchantment style, granting their wearer the instant ability to use both {@item longbow|XPHB|long} and {@item shortbow|XPHB|short bows} with the proficiency of someone who's been training since childhood. Indeed, each shot made while wearing them strikes with a little more force than usual, making them coveted by even trained archers.
That said, an even stronger magic comes from developing mastery of a skill through your own hard work and dedication. Knowledge that you've built inwardly will stay with you always.

This beginner's pattern will be a great step toward building your own skills with hook and stitch. The attractive design will develop your expertise with yarn-switching, while the laced closures makes the bracers adjustable for a range of sizes and functions.

images.title: Borders of the bracers.
Borders of the bracers with eyelets.

crochetPatternFluff.name: D20 Pillow
entries: Games of chance are popular among many adventurers, and for good reason. They help pass the time during quiet moments, build friendly bonds among party members, and might even earn the lucky winner a few extra coins.

It's little wonder that dice and cards are so well-liked among adventurers, given the role that fate plays in a quest's success. Regardless of proficiency or decades of training, there's always a touch of chaos during a mission. Learning to make the most of such mishaps is yet another part of an adventurer's training, one that can be surprisingly difficult to master.

This pattern lets you incorporate such chance into your everyday life in a soft and friendly manner. Whether you choose the small or large die, you can use it to answer such vital questions as "Whose turn is it to fetch snacks?"

crochetPatternFluff.name: Displacer Beast
entries: {@creature Displacer beast||Displacer beasts} are always disturbing to deal with. Those double tails with otherworldly suckers on the ends. Those six pairs of legs that leave behind only the faintest of marks to track. The way they are never, ever, where you think they're going to be! It's enough to make any {@class ranger} think about hanging up their bow for good.

They are beautiful, though, with the elegant grace of their silent prowl and the way their glossy fur catches the light rippling through the leaves. Not to mention their kittens! You've never seen anything quite as cute as two displacer beast cubs playing in fresh-fallen snow, blinking around and creating adorable duplicates with every pounce. This pattern captures that mischievous side in its full glory, from their lively expression to the frisky flick of their front paws.

images.title: Pin the Muzzle to the Head.
Pin the Nose to the Muzzle.
Pin the Eyelids to the Head.
This is what the wire should look like after assembling Tentacles Rows 2–5.
Add a stitch to close up the Tentacle.

crochetPatternFluff.name: Eye and Hand of Vecna
entries: The amigurumi arts need not be confined to living creatures. In this pattern, you will instead find undead artifacts of unspeakable power, all that now remains (hopefully!) of {@creature Vecna the Archlich|VEoR}. The powers of this legendary wizard-turned-undead remain imbued into his {@item eye of Vecna||eye} and {@item hand of Vecna||hand} after his defeat, and those who take up these artifacts can access some fraction of that power—at terrible cost, of course. Just as Vecna enjoyed luring adventurers into fiendish dungeons with the promise of great treasures, these artifacts continue his legacy of tempting would-be heroes to their doom. Those who bond with either item may gain powerful spells, strengths, and immunities, but will also acquire a warped morality, one that closely mirrors Vecna's own twisted soul.

Once these patterns are completed, be sure to make a careful inspection each time you approach your creations. The originals have been known to reappear in unexpected places and times. Accidentally attuning yourself to either one would be a great misfortune indeed.

crochetPatternFluff.name: Gelatinous Cube
entries: Proliferating in dark, confined spaces across the realms, these almost-translucent oozes remain a perennial problem for dungeon explorers. As if keeping your senses alert for every hint of a shadowed movement, odd smells, strange noises, hidden traps, and secret doors weren't enough, gelatinous cubes require you to watch out for faint wobbles in the air and suspiciously clean stonework. The only thing more irritating than trying to see one is failing and being engulfed by something that feels like lukewarm acidic butter. At least, before it begins to dissolve your socks and the skin underneath.

This plushie pattern will melt your heart, not your flesh. Its design incorporates several objects of the type that many gelatinous cubes carry around, such as bones and weapons. The size is just right to fit into a {@item dungeoneer's pack|XPHB}, and the design's challenges will keep both mind and fingers alert during those midnight watches.

crochetPatternFluff.name: Gloves of Missile Snaring
entries: In an ideal realm, this magical item would be utterly useless, because you'd never have a day when missiles were being hurled at you. Unfortunately, dodging missiles is a regrettably common activity for many adventurers. Every job comes with its professional hazards.

Luckily, {@item gloves of missile snaring} help with deflecting a wide range of {@item arrow|XPHB|arrows}, {@item bolt|XPHB|crossbow bolts}, {@item spear|XPHB|spears}, {@item javelin|XPHB|javelins}, and other thrown weapons. Occasionally, they'll even let you catch the weapon in question, giving you a chance to either add it to your stash or throw it back in the direction it came from.

This pattern's distinctive spiderweb design will encourage your opponents to think twice before launching pointy objects in your direction, and the warm weave will help keep your hands nimble enough to react quickly whenever danger comes your way.

crochetPatternFluff.name: Mantle of Spell Resistance
entries: You need not be a spellcaster to take advantage of this item's magical properties, which is why you can safely refute any {@class sorcerer}, {@class warlock}, or other arcanist who insists that it should automatically belong to their share of the quest loot. Anyone who slips the dark navy folds of this enchanted cloak over their shoulders can properly attune it, gaining immediate advantage when it comes to resisting any unwanted spells thrown their way. Fortunately, the magic is clever enough to tell the difference between helpful and harmful spells, so you needn't remove it every time you need {@spell cure wounds} to keep you in the fight.

Beyond its arcane advantages, this charming mantle cuts a chic silhouette for both city and wilderness wear. The distinctive gray edging and scalloped hem will add flair to both your traveling clothes and fancy attire, making it suitable for any occasion. Best of all, the wide lapel can be flipped up as needed to further protect your neck from both icy chills and {@spell ray of frost}.

crochetPatternFluff.name: Mimic Dice Bag
entries: A favorite topic of good-natured boasting when adventurers meet is comparing the strangest form of {@creature mimic} that they've encountered. These quirky creatures are renowned for their ability to take on different shapes—a hunting tactic that, while passive, seems to be quite effective, considering the continued expansion of both individual mimics and their colonies across various realms.

Although doors and chests are their most common forms, mimics are capable of imitating nearly any inanimate object. Indeed, particularly clever mimics have been rumored to take on the appearance of mimic-styled objects, tricking bypassers who assume that something shaped like a mimic couldn't possibly be one.

This pattern draws on all these traits to produce a charming little chest perfect for storing tiny treasures like gaming dice. The soft teeth allow for easy access, while the watchful eye will make others second-guess before reaching inside themselves.

crochetPatternFluff.name: Mind Flayer
entries: Thousands of years ago, the illithids ruled a vast empire that sprawled across the Astral Sea. They were the scourge of universes, using their nautiloid vessels to cross the planes of existence and capture intelligent humanoids from hundreds of worlds. The illithids fed on the brains of these captives, inspiring the name by which they are commonly known today: {@creature mind flayer||mind flayers}.
None would have dared challenge them in those days, let alone depict their alien physique in such squishy dimensions.

Though the grip of their astral tyranny has been broken, every adventurer should acquaint themselves with the illithids' key traits—bulbous skull, sleek tentacles, purple hue, and piercing black eyes—and remain alert for signs of their renewed activity. This pattern will both familiarize you with their physical form and produce an adorable toy, sure to inflict psychic damage on any mind flayer who comes across it.

crochetPatternFluff.name: Minsc's Hat of Winter Warming
entries: The Rashemen ranger {@creature Minsc and Boo!|MaBJoV|Minsc} has become beloved across Faerun's Sword Coast (and beyond!) for his bravery, frankness, and dedication to smashing evil wherever it may appear. The bardic tales of his exploits never fail to make note of his signature style: his commanding stature, his hamster companion, {@creature Minsc and Boo!|MaBJoV|Boo}, and the unmistakable purple tattoo that covers his bald scalp.

While this pattern can't grant you Minsc's size (or hamster), it will impart some of his warmth and visual panache as you pursue your wintery adventures. (Should you require the former, you might try an elixir of the colossus; the latter is nearly impossible to replicate, given that Boo is reportedly the only miniaturized {@creature giant space hamster|BAM} in existence.)

Wearing this distinctive hat will proudly communicate your commitment to kicking evil's butt wherever you may find it—and warn wrongdoers that, should they dare to commit crime in your presence, you will not hesitate to go for the eyes!

crochetPatternFluff.name: Owlbear Cub
entries: {@creature Owlbear||Owlbears} might well have inspired the phrase "dangerously cute." They're ferocious predators with sharp beaks, nasty claws, and a quick temper. Their lairs smell like rotting meat. They're famously aggressive and they enjoy sleeping on the bones of their devoured prey.

Despite all that, you'd be hard-pressed to find an adventurer who doesn't harbor a soft spot for these mysterious creatures. Perhaps it's those downy feathers that look like they'd be oh-so-soft to touch. Maybe it's the idea of dozing off beside one, fingers sinking into their warm coat. Possibly it's the hope of raising one to be a loyal companion, friendly and fierce in equal measure. (Note: Doing so may result in losing a finger or three, so consider yourself warned.)

Whatever the magic behind their appeal, there are few adventurers who would turn down an owlbear of their own, especially one that's safe to snuggle with. This pattern delivers all the cuteness with none of the biting.

crochetPatternFluff.name: Platinum Dragon Cowl
entries: The Platinum Dragon is one of many names for {@deity Bahamut|Nonhuman|PHB}, the founder and patron of the metallic dragons. He now resides among the Seven Heavens of Mount Celestia and walks the line between immortality and divinity. His devotees, both draconic and mortal, embrace his virtues of honor and justice. It's rumored that, in the face of extreme evil and with the offering of a great sacrifice, his followers can summon his aide to the material realms. In these moments, Bahamut manifests as the {@creature Aspect of Bahamut|FTD|Platinum Dragon}, a resplendent form that towers over even the most ancient of dragons. His glittering scales are a sight only the rarest and most worthy of adventurers will ever behold, invoking Bahamut's strength and mercy within the physical planes.

This pattern channels the awe and beauty of Bahamut's appearance in such moments. The soft choker band is within the skills of even a fledgling, while the dazzling scales add luster and visual complexity. This scene-stealing piece is sure to inspire the respect and admiration of all who behold its majesty, as is appropriate for the King of Metallic Dragons.

images.title: Attaching scales to the Cowl.
Attaching the rest of the scales.

crochetPatternFluff.name: Purple Worm Scarf
entries: One of the most amazing, terrifying things in the Underdark is the {@creature purple worm}. These gargantuan worms are capable of burrowing through solid rock, mindlessly consuming everything in their path. They can swallow a horse (or a human) in a single chomp, leaving their unlucky meal trapped inside their acid-filled stomach for eventual digestion.

As if that's not enough, purple worms love a good party. They're attracted to noise of all kinds and will crash any rowdy gathering faster than you can scream "Run!" Since they digest the rocks that they gnaw through, they leave behind wide tunnels that can wind for miles underground.

This scarf evokes the purple worm's power in the face of frozen weather, carving a tunnel of warmth around your neck. Its stylish flair will attract as much attention as the rumbling of a worm's approach, but this scarf will inspire admiration rather than spark terror.

images.title: Bobbles in tail.

crochetPatternFluff.name: Quiver of Ehlonna
entries: As the Goddess of Greyhawk's woodlands and forests, {@deity Ehlonna|Greyhawk|XDMG} holds a special appeal for any {@class ranger}, especially those who have studied at Stalwart Pines or wandered the paths of Celadon Forest. The {@item quiver of Ehlonna} is a blessing to any archer, for it holds a sizable inventory of arrows, bolts, and other ranged weapons within its small, light frame.

This pattern will empower you with a different, but no less vital, container, ensuring that you are never without easy hydration on your daily quests. This quiver is 9 inches (23 cm) high and 6 inches (15 cm) wide, holding a refillable water bottle of similar size or any comparable beverage container. Its solid bottom prevents slippage, while the intricate patterning is sure to catch the eye of fellow adventurers.

images.title: This is what the bag Strap will look like after Row 2.
{@note Follow this pattern after Round 10.}

crochetPatternFluff.name: Red Dragon
entries: None but a fool would dismiss the power, grandeur, and might of any dragon. Yet among this proud pantheon, the {@creature adult red dragon||red dragon} holds a special place because of the awe, reverence, and fear that it evokes. One would be no less dead if struck full-blast by a {@creature adult blue dragon||blue dragon's} lightning, a {@creature adult green dragon||green's} poison, or any of the other draconic breath weapons, but there is something in the threat of a {@creature adult red dragon||red dragon's} fire that sparks the imagination above all else.

Whatever the reason, red dragons are perhaps the most feared type among the commonfolk, and the most searched after by any adventurer yearning to be known as a "dragonslayer." This pattern will help you conquer this mighty creature in a new way, transforming its powerful wings and mighty scales into complex stitches that will show off the finest of your fiber skills. Just remember to use fireproof materials if you plan to place your finished masterpiece among its native flames—unlike its inspiration, this red dragon is regrettably lacking in fire immunity.

images.title: Subrow 94A, showing 20th sc marked with blue stitch marker.
Start of Subrow 94B, showing hook inserted into marked stitch with ch12 left on inside of Body.
End of Subrow 94B, showing 12sc (starting from marked stitch) and 14ch.
Start of 94C, showing chains inside.
These two photos show what your work should look like at the end of Rnd 94.

crochetPatternFluff.name: Schools of Magic Granny Squares
entries: Abjuration, conjuration, evocation, and more: The schools of magic take on different forms in different realms, but the concept of loosely dividing arcane spellwork into eight types is common across them all. No doubt, mystical experts such as Mordenkainen or Elminster could explain the magical underpinnings of this interdimensional phenomenon in excruciating detail, probably with cosmic illusions and a thousand-page reading list. Alas, such musings go well beyond the scope of a crafting book—although they could make for an interesting conversation while you work on these designs.

This beginner pattern will let you master all eight schools of magic at a speed that the students of {@book Strixhaven|SCC} would envy. The resulting squares can be used alone as simple coasters or stitched together for a throw blanket or wall hanging. Once mastered, you can use them to add a magical touch to other projects, embellishing anything you'd like with a dash of your favorite magic.

images.title: Abjuration
Necromancy
Illusion
Enchantment
Evocation
Transmutation
Conjuration
Divination

crochetPatternFluff.name: Soul Coin Coaster
entries: No adventurer of pure heart and good intent would want {@item soul coin|BGDIA|soul coins} cluttering up their home. These discs of infernal metal each bind a single soul, doomed to be traded among devils and fiends until freed or tragically consumed. The soul's emotions—despair, rage, grief—bleed through to anyone who touches them. Even untouched, they exude a discomforting aura, like being in the presence of a barely audible scream.

Coasters stitched to look like soul coins, on the other hand, are much more cheerful than the coins that inspired them. These handy objects will protect your furniture and provide the perfect opening to share tales of your own Avernus-related adventures. Their yarn construction is guaranteed to absorb only liquids, not souls.{@sup [{@footnote *1|Guarantee does not apply if soul coin coasters have been enchanted in any way. Not recommended for use under {@item potion of invisibility||potions of invisibility}, for reasons that should be easy to see.}]} In a pinch, they can be thrown to distract all manner of {@creature imp||imps} and lesser devils; combining one with a {@spell minor illusion} spell has been known to fool even a {@creature pit fiend}, at least temporarily.

images.title: Sketch the design on the stabilizer.
Once you've filled in the design, wash away the stabilizer.
Create 2 semicircles on either side of the central design.

crochetPatternFluff.name: Spellbook Pouch Belt Bag
entries: Every wizard knows the importance of keeping their {@item spellbook|PHB} and their spell components close at hand. This handy belt bag will allow you to do both in style. The scalloped fold provides an extra layer of padding for delicate items like rose petals or vials of pixie dust. The secure button and double hooks will encourage pickpockets to look for an easier target, and the decorative edging will keep your tome securely in place.

This pattern is sized for a standard traveling {@item spellbook|PHB}. Wizards who use a tome of exceptionally ornate size or decoration may need to make some modifications. I don't recommend using this bag to carry either the {@item fulminating treatise|TCE} or the {@item tome of the stilled tongue}: The former is likely to set the wool on fire and the latter is too large (and, honestly, too gross) to fit neatly inside.
